prompt: We discovered a database breach exposing 50,000 Brazilian customers names, emails, and encrypted passwords. What are our LGPD notification obligations and what should we do in the next 72 hours?
assertions: States the ANPD notification deadline correctly: 3 WORKING days from becoming aware, per ANPD Resolution CD/ANPD 15/2024 (not a GDPR-style 72 clock-hours), with the complete/supplementary report within 20 working days

Applies the Art. 48 trigger analysis: notification is required when the incident may cause relevant risk or damage to data subjects — weighing data types (names + emails exposed; passwords encrypted/hashed is a mitigating factor), scale (50,000 subjects), and potential harm (credential stuffing, phishing)

Requires notifying affected data subjects when relevant risk/damage exists (Art. 48), with practical content: what happened, what data was affected, measures taken, and user recommendations such as password resets

Specifies ANPD notification content aligned with Art. 48 §1º: nature of the affected data, data subjects involved, technical and security measures used, risks, and measures taken or planned to mitigate

The 72-hour action plan includes containment/eradication, forensic evidence preservation, forced credential invalidation/password resets, and documenting the incident internally even if assessed as non-notifiable (accountability principle; Res. 15/2024 record-keeping duty)
